question: C-002-bitemporal-feasibility
claim: CAUTION keystone. ~20 internal + 5 external reads SELECT FROM code_nodes/edges with no invalid_at filter; columns are additive (SCHEMA_VERSION 5->6 via ensureSchemaMigrations) but MUST land atomically WITH Q1-C1-views or every reader surfaces superseded rows as live. Needs Q6-C1 first.

question: C-002-bitemporal-feasibility
claim: CAUTION, co-ships with Q1-C1. Live views ARE the mitigation (WHERE invalid_at IS NULL = identical to base table when nothing closed out); mechanical repoint FROM code_nodes->code_nodes_live across ONLY <=6 files (seed-resolver, code-graph-context, ensure-ready, cross-file-edge-resolver + db internal) so grep proves completeness. Missed repoint = stale-row regression (test-caught, not silent corruption).
evidence: grep consumers = 4 external files + db internal queries

question: C-002-bitemporal-feasibility
claim: CAUTION, after Q1-C1+views. Edge readers + bulk edge DELETEs (replaceEdges :985, prune :1011-1034, removeFile :1149) become close-outs; dangling-prune must re-express against the LIVE node set or it closes edges to still-live nodes; deferDanglingTargetPrune :957-968 order-sensitivity transfers into close-out logic.

question: C-002-bitemporal-feasibility
claim: NEEDS-BENCHMARK, LAST. The in-place resolver UPDATE SET target_id (:91-95) + retainedSourceIds guard make resolved state = f(assertions + mutation order); must move to close-old+assert-new (or computed live view). Re-deriving resolution over the full assertion set each scan/read is the perf unknown. High blast, reversible-by-rebuild.

question: C-002-bitemporal-feasibility
claim: 4-step rollout: (1) Q6-C1 generation GO standalone -> (2) Q1-C1 columns + views as ONE atomic SCHEMA_VERSION 5->6 (keystone) -> (3) edge-lifecycle -> (4) CRDT-merge gated on benchmark.
